role_in_text: developer of the photonic quantum chip
occurrence_sentence: At the time of its development the chip represented one of the most advanced integrated photonic quantum circuits created in Europe, helping establish photonics as a serious platform for the future of quantum computing. mentions.role_in_text: Introduces PACU, a Photonic Assembly Control Unit for scalable quantum systems
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum today introduced PACU, its new Photonic Assembly Control Unit designed to provide a scalable, standardized control layer for the company's photonic quantum systems and future universal quantum computers.

mentions.role_in_text: Introduces PACU, a Photonic Assembly Control Unit for scalable quantum systems
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum today introduced PACU, its new Photonic Assembly Control Unit designed to provide a scalable, standardized control layer for the company's photonic quantum systems and future universal quantum computers.

mentions.role_in_text: Designed and introduced the PACU, a photonic assembly control unit.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: The unit is designed to host photonic chips with up to 1,000 low-speed and up to 32 high-speed phase shifters, giving QuiX Quantum the control infrastructure needed to operate complex universal photonic architectures and integrate that capability into a compact, rack-mountable system.

mentions.role_in_text: developer of photonic quantum computing systems
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum has positioned photonics as the basis for its universal quantum computing roadmap, with systems designed for modularity, data-center compatibility, and integration into hybrid quantum-classical computing environments.
"As photonic quantum chips become more capable, the systems around them must scale as well," said Stefan Hengesbach, CEO of QuiX Quantum.

By combining rack-based integration, air cooling, monitoring, protection, and support for large photonic assemblies, PACU gives QuiX Quantum a repeatable control architecture for its current photonic chips and future universal quantum computing systems.

QuiX Quantum, the Dutch photonic quantum computing pioneer, today announced it has secured €15 million in Series A funding to deliver the world's first single-photon-based universal quantum computer in 2026.

10 July 2025 – QuiX Quantum, the Dutch photonic quantum computing pioneer, today announced it has secured €15 million in Series A funding to deliver the world's first single-photon-based universal quantum computer in 2026.

mentions.role_in_text: Developer and installer of the Feed-Forward Control Unit for universal photonic quantum computing.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum today announced the first installation of its Feed-Forward Control Unit (FFCU), a high-performance hardware component developed for the company's universal photonic quantum computing architecture.

mentions.role_in_text: Developer and installer of the Feed-Forward Control Unit (FFCU) for universal photonic quantum computing.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum today announced the first installation of its Feed-Forward Control Unit (FFCU), a high-performance hardware component developed for the company's universal photonic quantum computing architecture.

The FFCU is part of QuiX Quantum's broader quantum computing architecture, which brings together photon generation, multiplexing, state generation, measurement, photonic assembly control and feed-forward control into a single photonic quantum computing stack.

mentions.role_in_text: provider of photonic quantum computing hardware
mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum's FFCU combines FPGA-based digital processing with a custom analog front-end to support deterministic control of Mach-Zehnder interferometers on integrated photonic circuits.

mentions.occurrence_sentence: QuiX Quantum is a leading provider of photonic quantum computing hardware driving innovation across Europe in the development of its Universal Quantum Computer.
